title: Skills
subtitle: Processes the AI should learn — both internal and external (legal, accounting, vendors)
intro: A "skill" is a process the AI can run when you ask. Drafting a wholesale follow-up. Building a monthly report. Writing a return-merchandise reply. List the ones worth teaching — internal first, then anything you outsource to legal / accounting / etc. that touches your work.

steps.title: Internal processes
steps.intro: Things your team does — repetitive enough to be worth teaching the AI.

itemFields.label: Skill name
itemFields.placeholder: "Draft a wholesale follow-up" / "Build the weekly retail report"

itemFields.label: When does this happen?
itemFields.placeholder: Every Monday morning / when a wholesale account hasn't ordered in 90 days / after every return is processed

itemFields.label: What info comes IN?
itemFields.placeholder: Account name, last order date, what they usually buy, their rep's name

itemFields.label: What gets produced?
itemFields.placeholder: A 4-paragraph email draft, ready for the rep to send. Or a CSV. Or a Slack message.

itemFields.label: How is it done today, step by step?
itemFields.placeholder:
1. Pull the account's order history from Shopify
2. Note their usual cadence
3. Check if anything's been said in the last 30 days
4. Draft something warm, not pushy — mention something specific to their store
5. Sign with the rep's name
itemFields.help: The steps are the skill. The AI follows them.

itemFields.label: What's painful about doing this today?
itemFields.placeholder: It's the kind of thing that gets pushed to Fridays and then doesn't happen.

itemFields.label: How should the AI help? How much oversight does it need?
itemFields.placeholder: Draft only — a person reviews and sends. / Run the data pull and surface the list, the rep writes the email. / Fully automate after the first month of human approval.

steps.title: External processes
steps.intro: Things you outsource to professionals — legal, accounting, vendors — where the AI should help your team interact with them, not replace them.

itemFields.label: Skill name
itemFields.placeholder: "Prep questions for the quarterly meeting with accounting" / "Draft a vendor scope-of-work"

itemFields.label: Who's the outside party?
itemFields.placeholder: Our accountant (Cerity Partners) / our lawyer / our packaging vendor

itemFields.label: How is it done today, step by step?
How should the AI help?
itemFields.placeholder: Draft the agenda / prep the brief. A person reviews before anything goes to the external party.

steps.title: What we should NOT automate
steps.intro: The judgment-heavy stuff. Worth being explicit so it stays out of scope.

fields.label: Processes the AI should NOT touch
fields.placeholder:
One per line:
- Anything that goes to a customer who's complained twice
- Pricing decisions on wholesale terms
- Final approval on creative campaigns
- Anything related to staff or hiring
fields.help: Write these as workflows, not vague principles. "Don't price wholesale accounts" beats "don't make business decisions."
